7b077caa52c14723: Forms, responses, and API usage in one place.
dd329cda2cf640fe: Create one trackable item, choose how it collects data, and review every submission or usage event in one dashboard.
e7e8040fe67dabe3: Everything you need to track what matters
449ddf87832b17bd: One platform for collecting and reviewing form responses, API usage events, and AI-powered insights — no duct tape required.
0651a2901f7f5a75: Create custom forms with ratings, booleans, and free-text fields. Share a public link and collect responses instantly.
a64e7dcccaeed45d: Send usage events and structured metadata directly from your application using a secure API key — no SDK required.
274d07a9cb04ce62: View submission trends, event counts, and a rolling 7-day activity chart across all your trackable items.
9092d3cd7fb6dcc7: Connect Claude, ChatGPT, or any MCP-compatible AI tool to query your data, search logs, and summarize responses.
985ffdb7bb3926c8: Organize trackable items into separate workspaces and invite team members with role-based access control.
598330d21d3a42c0: Fully open-source under a permissive license. Deploy to your own infrastructure in minutes using Docker Compose.
d7764b683a44ba59: Create an item, decide how data is collected, and review it in one unified dashboard.
d9b9bf87c90f620f: Start with a title, description, and the fields or events you want to track. Each item generates a secure destination.
c743f22544154f8a: Share a clean, dynamic form for manual submissions, or use a secure API key to automatically ingest events and metadata.
7b85324a55a25de9: Monitor event history, aggregate counts, and structured feedback from a single, unified dashboard view.
f0c03ddd3395d724: A flexible platform for any data workflow
a0101f5efd780b4d: Stop switching between different tools for surveys, feedback forms, and product analytics. Trackables unifies everything using a consistent collection model.
1b7e71a2f49420eb: Easily capture ratings, booleans, unstructured text, and more from customizable public forms.
9902a741dea62498: Integrate deeply into your application to log background events and track granular product usage.
b067c940acb635ad: Instantly view all historical submissions and raw usage counts without managing fragmented dashboards.
6d6f3727fa7ae2ef: Trackables ships with a built-in MCP server, letting tools like Claude and ChatGPT query your data, search logs, and analyze survey responses — no custom integration required.
92b75c92dfeb2ff3: Ask your AI assistant to find specific events, filter by date or field value, and surface patterns across thousands of log entries — all in plain language.
68b4dc8ec762c454: “Find all errors logged in the last 7 days”
dc03c36a6ec8b9a4: Let AI read and interpret open-ended survey responses at scale. Identify positive trends, recurring complaints, and key themes without reading every reply.
b8d1cff44fda6c2f: “What are customers saying about our checkout flow?”
05908ddd257d5b9f: Generate concise summaries of your survey results or usage reports on demand. Share AI-written digests with your team directly from your chat interface.
e6d25d2c3e1b9a3d: “Summarize this week's NPS responses into a report”
58f37d4ac07dee1e: Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and any tool that supports the Model Context Protocol can connect to your Trackables data in seconds.
0285155cd3925d9b: Trackables is fully open-source. For teams requiring strict data ownership or isolated network deployments, self-host in minutes using Docker.

7875c035d2d57b7e: This file controls runtime product behavior — plan limits, auth email delivery, billing, webhooks, and feature flags. It is separate from
c11e44993dd20364: , which holds secrets and infrastructure wiring.

25e9b1005b1dcead: An array of email addresses that are granted site admin access. Admins can access privileged pages such as the batch process scheduler and system-wide usage dashboards.
93343060a20b517f: List of email addresses with admin access. Must match the email associated with the user's Trackables account.
3a6d1685056d22c0: Auth-specific runtime settings. Email delivery is opt-in and uses a Redis-backed queue rather than direct SMTP delivery from the app process.
08e90d7bc89c9c50: When true, auth emails are rendered with React Email and enqueued into Redis for a separate sender process or sideloaded worker. When false, verification, password reset, and change-email flows are disabled.
ad08e7cdd948ca4a: Feature flags that control which parts of the product UI are active. For simple self-hosted deployments, you can disable all billing features and leave only the scheduler flag if needed.
2f6fd461c3c5ac81: When true, users can view and upgrade to paid plans. When false, the upgrade UI shows a billing coming soon placeholder instead.
1e621eebc09394a0: When true, billing tiers and plan information appear in the sidebar and workspace settings. Set to false to hide all billing UI entirely.
a717c106e82854c9: When true, the batch process scheduler is active and admin-accessible. Requires Redis and BullMQ to be running.
099a60231847aa43: When false, the MCP server authenticates using the built-in OAuth bearer tokens issued by Trackables. When true, self-managed API tokens are used instead — useful for environments where you want MCP access to bypass the built-in OAuth flow.
7c172daaa4b79308: Controls which webhook provider types are available. Set to true to enable all providers (generic, Microsoft Teams, Discord), false to disable webhooks entirely, or an array of provider names to enable a specific subset.
4248ffac941d2c20: An array of limit sets that control resource usage. Each entry can be tied to a billing tier via
52c96ec58e0fa6a8: to apply to all users regardless of plan. For simple deployments with no billing, a single entry with
f054717289c3c8ab: values for numeric limits mean unlimited.
9b2c9fdad2745eff: Unique identifier for this limit set (e.g. free, pro, default).
a24221d8629a7935: Maximum number of trackable items (forms + API endpoints) a user can create per workspace.
eb6064416510649d: Maximum number of responses allowed per survey/form. null means unlimited.
e1422c030cf5c1dd: Maximum number of members in a workspace. null means unlimited.
cb32c667be7a5ae2: Maximum number of log events that can be ingested per minute per trackable item. Excess requests are rate-limited.
83909cd87fc00447: Maximum size in bytes of a single log payload. Requests larger than this are rejected.
9c81bcda92ea923a: Number of days log entries are retained before being purged. Applies to all logs within this tier.
539baaa20869d2ed: Maximum number of workspaces a single user can create. null means unlimited.
3a32369f26d9c82c: The billing tier ID this limit applies to. Must match an id in the billing.tiers array. Set to null to apply this limit to all users regardless of plan.
a66a85b427f74364: Optional billing configuration for Lemon Squeezy integration. If you are not using paid plans, set
9b1df5435e7f2025: as an empty array. Tier id values are referenced by limits[].billingTier — they must match exactly.
94fb34dbb474cf51: Your Lemon Squeezy store ID. Required if you are processing payments through Lemon Squeezy.
d42e4c8bc5eeba3c: URL to the billing management page shown to users. Typically your Lemon Squeezy customer portal URL.
ac41506d518d8878: Array of billing tiers displayed in the upgrade UI. Each tier must have a unique id that matches a corresponding entry in limits[].billingTier.
571227823067cf84: Unique tier identifier. Referenced by limits[].billingTier.
49ddd61a7671d43e: Display name shown to users (e.g. Pro, Team).
ad51c78b271835a1: Price string shown in the UI (e.g. $25).
8b7345f745d99655: Billing interval shown below the price (e.g. per month).
64fc4ef3f81d436c: Short description of what this tier includes.
e2f9a7063999d175: Visual theme of the tier card. accent and strong are highlighted styles; neutral is the default.
67a9d1da1abb9d0d: When true, shows a Most Popular badge on this tier card.
4500c9bbadd7091e: Lemon Squeezy product variant ID for this tier. Required for checkout to work.
39137601bf49fe59: When false, this tier is hidden from the upgrade UI even if billing is enabled.
e22f1e366071a966: Controls the outbound webhook delivery queue backed by Redis and BullMQ. Rate limiting here applies to the queue consumer, not to inbound log ingestion.
3919a0bb6ec2db96: When true, outbound webhooks are queued in Redis and delivered asynchronously. Requires Redis to be running.
1c29fbd72fe48cfa: Minimum milliseconds to wait between consecutive webhook deliveries from the queue.
47f6a7513b726c15: Maximum number of webhook events that can be sent within the rateLimitMs window.
c21f68f8b9cfa890: Configuration for the background batch scheduler. The scheduler must also be enabled via
e012e0d1897d6374: IANA timezone string used for scheduling batch jobs (e.g. UTC, America/New_York). Affects when cron-based jobs trigger.
